title: Bardiel VS Shinji Wheres his brain gone
display_title: Bardiel VS Shinji Wheres his brain gone
wikitext:
[]: Bardiel VS. Shinji: Where's his brain gone?             Quote Post by tehprognoob » Sat Dec 25, 2010 7:00 pm In the fight with Bardiel, if he'd moved a bit, resisted and cut the webbing around the Plug, he'd have saved Touji SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sat Dec 25, 2010 7:10 pm Because he's absolutely terrified and doesn't have time to think out the possible outcomes, because he has no idea that his father can seize control away from him if he doesn't act quickly enough, and because unfortunately for his victimized friend, the plot demands it.

[]:             Quote Post by tehprognoob » Sat Dec 25, 2010 7:13 pm Baka Shinji...

[]:             Quote Post by Qaiz » Sat Dec 25, 2010 8:54 pm As Esselfortium said, it is easy for us to view the clip and assume he could of done things differently. But being a 14 year old boy, and with the weight of the world as well as his farther upon him, he couldn't of possibly thought straight enough as to do something strategic. He was shocked that this eva unit was possessed as it was. If we assume that he could of done things differently in this fight, couldn't we assume that everything in eva could of been different? Plus, it wouldn't of been as awesome a scene as it was if he did so

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Dec 25, 2010 9:10 pm Forget Shinji. Nobody at the Command Center so much as mentions the possibility of removing the plug. Having the item brought up, and then struck down by Gendo or whoever else ("The Angel appears the be concentrated in the plug; we can't risk exposing the Evas to contamination!" etc.), early in the operation would have made the later situation seem a bit less contrived.

[]:             Quote Post by Qaiz » Sat Dec 25, 2010 9:21 pm True. Plus Im sure it wasn't stuck on lose or anything, even trying to eject the entry plug proved futile. Plus it was a great excuse to use the dummy plug.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Dec 25, 2010 9:41 pm Reichu wrote: Forget Shinji. Nobody at the Command Center so much as mentions the possibility of removing the plug.

[]:             Quote Post by Cody MacArthur Fett » Sat Dec 25, 2010 9:42 pm Reichu wrote: Forget Shinji. Nobody at the Command Center so much as mentions the possibility of removing the plug. Having the item brought up, and then struck down by Gendo or whoever else ("The Angel appears the be concentrated in the plug; we can't risk exposing the Evas to contamination!" etc.), early in the operation would have made the later situation seem a bit less contrived.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Dec 25, 2010 9:48 pm NemZ wrote: Didn't Gendo order them to eject the plug almost as soon as trouble started to no avail? That's how they first realized it was an angel, and I assume that's also why Rei was targeting the plug rather than the core.

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Sun Dec 26, 2010 3:40 am I've wanted to comment on this topic for a while now. There's simply no getting around it. The entire battle against Bardiel just flat out pisses me off. Qaiz wrote: ...being a 14 year old boy, and with the weight of the world as well as his farther upon him, he couldn't of possibly thought straight enough as to do something strategic.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Dec 26, 2010 4:28 am BC Baron wrote: Sadly, with Misato out of commission in Matsushiro, any semblance of strategy from the commander was sorely missing that day.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Sun Dec 26, 2010 7:11 am Mr. Tines wrote: Really, this is what it all hinges on -- she may come up with some crazy sounding schemes -- "Pilots -- catch that falling Angel!" -- which work and tried to keep them operating as a team; but on that day there was no tactical cohesion, and plenty of bickering between the pilots.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Sun Dec 26, 2010 7:13 am Since the question posed in this thread is about Shinji's inability to rescue the pilot from Unit-03, let's disregard Gendo's tactical ineptitude and focus on the options Shinji had by the time he was facing off against Bardiel. One thing often overlooked: once he stops his menacing, kaiju like lumbering, Unit-03 is agile, and damn fast. Possibly the fastest moving opponent Shinji had to face in the entire series. And that's without getting into the stretchy arms deal. I think it's pretty safe to say that he was simply nowhere near skilled enough to take down Bardiel on his own. That's one thing i prefer about the NME version of this battle: since teamwork was out of the question, the engagement didn't have to take several turns to get to it's conclusion (though i would argue that, contrived as it may have been, the Bardiel battle of NGE was great at buildup).

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Sun Dec 26, 2010 7:35 am I'd also like to bring forth the issue with the fact that they have no reliable way to tell if pilot is still synchronized enough for feedback (and to what extent) inside Eva-03. Given that Bardiel only stops after Eva-03's neck is broken, I'm not entirely sure if Shinji could have brought himself to actually stop Bardiel enough to carve that entry plug out even if he was ordered to do so. Because he might fear causing fatal damage to the pilot in the process. Then again, if he knew about the dummy system and what it would do, and given some coaxing along the lines "you can try doing it and have a chance at actually saving your friend or give up and ensure s/he gets totally shafted, those are your options", things could have been different. But drama required it this way to break things up beyond repair between Shinji and Gendo and I also got the feeling Gendo was simply being trigger-happy to give his new toy a field test...

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Dec 26, 2010 8:14 am I think you're all being to hard on Gendo - he tried to eject the plug and everything and the Dummy sytem was unstable and couldn't be controlled properly - all they could do is turn it on or turn it off SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by tehprognoob » Sun Dec 26, 2010 10:40 am suggestions in a tactical nerd moment: In case of NGE: two evas draw back while firing suppressive fire and holding out max AT Field. Unit 02, the most skilledly piloted sneak up from behing using the mountains as cover while units 01 and 00 really go berserk on bardiel, then unit 02 jumps him from the back and cut the web and units 01 and 00 move to restrain bardiel. Asuka hauls ass and then comes back with some big weapon and they mob the angel together. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Sun Dec 26, 2010 9:05 pm Someone’s probably already said this in some form or another, but Shinji panics and more or less goes into vapor lock mode and refuses to do anything. The whole point is he’s not thinking logically or else he would’ve realized one person is not worth the entirety of humanity (or just rip out the plug). But Shinji’s not some strategic far thinking person; he’s Shinji and thus things go to hell very quickly.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sun Dec 26, 2010 9:11 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Assasinate » Mon Dec 27, 2010 1:54 am well if you are going to die in an instant, would you still have time to think??

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Dec 27, 2010 9:50 am Cody MacArthur Fett wrote: Indeed. This is just one of those scenes that keeps Eva from from the top slots in terms of "greatest anime."
